frozen_components: Host LLM (used for decoding and synthetic NLQ generation)
architecture.summary: TeCoD is a Text-to-SQL system that addresses accuracy and latency challenges by converting historical NL-SQL pairs into reusable templates and enforcing them during generation. The system has three main components: (1) Template extraction and indexing, which masks literals in SQL and NLQs and creates vector embeddings; (2) Template selection, which uses a fine-tuned NLI model to match incoming user queries to stored templates with high precision; (3) SQL generation via partitioned grammar-constrained decoding, which pre-compiles template grammars into flexible formats and generates only query-specific literals at inference time while reusing static token sequences. Information flows from labeled workload data through template extraction, indexed for retrieval by semantic similarity and NLI entailment checking, then constrains LLM decoding via grammar-guided token masking and a two-phase partitioned strategy that separates query-invariant static parts from query-specific slots.
trained_components: NLI template matcher model (fine-tuned from pre-trained BERT-like NLI)
Template grammar compilation and flexible grammar generation

biblio.title: Reliable Answers for Recurring Questions: Boosting Text-to-SQL Accuracy with Template Constrained Decoding
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data, Volume 3, Issue 6, 2025, Article 357, Pages 1 - 26

claims.dataset: BIRD and Spider (synthetic augmented, matched query subset)
claims.statement: TeCoD yields up to 36% higher execution accuracy than in-context learning (ICL) on matched queries
TeCoD achieves close to 90% execution accuracy on matched queries across almost all databases, compared to much lower and variable accuracy with ZeroShot and ICL
2.2x lower latency on matched queries with TeCoD
Template matcher accuracy improved from 73% to 91% through strategies of casting matching as NLI, masking literal parts of questions, and generating synthetic paraphrases
Real workload analysis of a very large bank shows that workloads contain clusters of highly similar SQLs, motivating the template-based approach

BIRD-dev set contains 61 templates covering 134 queries out of 1534 total queries; Spider-dev set contains 482 templates covering 966 queries out of 1034 total queries in real (non-synthesized) benchmark data

Flexible template grammar (allowing case variations, whitespace flexibility, and SQL formatting alternatives) improves accuracy over fixed string-based template constraints by accommodating different LLM formatting preferences

In-context learning disappoints in how well it harnesses related queries; LLMs can fail to generate correct SQL even in the presence of an in-context example with SQL differing only by a constant (demonstrated by two concrete examples)
